fields.description:
Wire the two-scope gate posture across the three gates, plumb resolution-class + sanitized diagnostic through the two verdict-returning gates, define the exit-11 contract, own the shared-file (runner.py) module-size reconciliation, enforce never-certify structurally, wrap the MCP failure surface, and document the model. Lands LAST (wave 3); INTEGRATION OWNER for the seams the other children touch.

## Why
The three gates must share a systemic *block-but-retryable* posture while PRESERVING per-criterion fail-open, carry `resolution_class` + `diagnostic` so routing and troubleshooting both work, expose a distinct-but-grounded exit code, never sign a degraded result, and tell the human exactly what to do.

## What

### Two-scope posture
A **systemic** failure ŌåÆ block-but-retryable (exit 11, unsigned) across the gates; a **per-criterion** failure ŌåÆ drop-and-continue fail-open (one flaky finder among N must NOT block a claim ŌĆö PRESERVED, regression-tested). Make code-review match plan-review's existing systemic block; turn completion's fail-closed-as-FAIL into a distinct retryable exit (NOT a verification FAIL, which means "your work is wrong").

### The single source of retryability: `LLMOutcome`
mamba's `classify_llm_failure` returns an `LLMOutcome` with `resolution_class` (the closed 8-value enum) AND a pre-computed `retryable: bool`. Everything downstream reuses BOTH ŌĆö the write-sites persist both into `coverage`; the CLIs read the persisted `retryable` bool directly and never re-derive retryability by string-comparing the class name.

### Two data-flow shapes
- **Shape A ŌĆö plan-review + code-review return a DEGRADED VERDICT DICT.**
  - **Write-site:** `gate_dispatch._degraded_plan_review_verdict` / `_degraded_code_review_verdict` read `outcome = getattr(error, 'outcome', None)` off the caught `error` and, WHEN PRESENT, set `coverage['resolution_class'] = outcome.resolution_class.value`, `coverage['diagnostic'] = outcome.diagnostic`, and `coverage['retryable'] = outcome.retryable`.
  - **Reachability (verified):** `produce_plan_review_verdict` calls the helper three ways ŌĆö the two `preflight`/mid-run `LLMUnavailableError` paths pass the **exception object** (`.outcome` present ŌåÆ disposition set); the finders-failed tail passes `res.error` as a **string** (no `.outcome` ŌåÆ NO disposition ŌåÆ a plain INDETERMINATE, exit 2). That is correct: a finders-produced-nothing failure is not classified transient. So disposition is carried on exactly the genuine-outage paths.
  - **code-review translation:** `rebar.llm.review_code` returns a `review_result` TRANSLATED from the gate `code_review_verdict` by `shim._verdict_to_review_result`. That translator is updated to PRESERVE `coverage.resolution_class` / `coverage.retryable` / `coverage.diagnostic` onto the returned `review_result` so the CLI can read them.
- **Shape B ŌĆö completion-verify FAILS by RAISING `LLMUnavailableError`** (no verdict dict). `transition_close._completion_precheck` (line ~174) has a broad `except Exception as exc: raise CommandError(...) from None` that TODAY discards the exception + its `.outcome`. Fix (SINGLE mechanism ŌĆö no subclass, no new attribute): in `_completion_precheck`, before building the message, compute `_o = getattr(exc, 'outcome', None)` and `rc = 11 if (_o and _o.retryable) else 1`, then `raise CommandError(<class-specific message>, returncode=rc) from None`. `CommandError` already carries `returncode`, and `transition.py` already returns `exc.returncode` to the process ŌĆö so exit 11 propagates with no plumbing change. The shape-B path also writes the sanitized diagnostic to the session log (see below).

### Exit-code-11 contract
Retryable (`_o.retryable`, i.e. `WAIT_AND_RETRY`/`RETRY_NOW`) ŌåÆ exit **11** ("transient ŌĆö retry", NEW). Any other non-null disposition ŌåÆ the gate's EXISTING INDETERMINATE exit. Null disposition ŌåÆ the existing PASS/BLOCK exit.
- **Exit 2 is NOT redefined.** `review-plan` ALREADY exits 2 for INDETERMINATE (pinned by arawana); UNCHANGED. Only the retryable subset is peeled to 11.
- **CLI sites (`_llm_commands.py`):** `_review_plan()` (shape A; tail currently `0/1/2` ŌĆö extended to add 11) and `_review_code()` (shape A; TODAY always returns 0 on any verdict ŌĆö updated to distinguish `0/1/2/11` off the preserved `coverage`). Shape B surfaces through TWO CLI entrypoints: `rebar verify-completion` (`_verify_completion()`) AND the `rebar close` / `rebar transition ŌĆ” closed` path (which reaches `close_ticket` ŌåÆ `_completion_precheck`; `transition.py` returns the `CommandError.returncode`, so exit 11 flows there too). `_review()` (the per-ticket `review_ticket` advisory reviewer) is NOT a site.
- **Message delivery:** the class-specific message (table below) is printed to STDERR ŌĆö shape B as the `CommandError` message; shape A as a CLI-rendered line off `coverage`.
- **Worked example:** a 429 degraded plan-review carries `coverage.retryable=True, resolution_class='WAIT_AND_RETRY'` ŌåÆ `_review_plan` exits 11; a 429 during `rebar close` raises `LLMUnavailableError`, `_completion_precheck` reads `.outcome.retryable` ŌåÆ `CommandError(returncode=11)` ŌåÆ the close exits 11 (fail-closed, unsigned).

### Per-class actionable messages (ResolutionClass ŌåÆ stderr text)
| ResolutionClass | Message |
|---|---|
| `WAIT_AND_RETRY` | Transient provider overload/rate-limit ŌĆö wait for the backoff window, then retry. |
| `RETRY_NOW` | Transient connection blip ŌĆö retry immediately. |
| `INCREASE_PROVIDER_LIMITS` | Provider usage/quota ceiling hit ŌĆö raise the provider limit or wait for the reset. |
| `CHANGE_SETTINGS` | The request exceeded a configured bound (tokens/steps/timeout) ŌĆö adjust it and retry. |
| `CHANGE_INPUT` | The input was rejected (too large / malformed) ŌĆö reduce or fix it and retry. |
| `CHANGE_PROVIDER_OR_MODEL` | The model/provider is unavailable or refused ŌĆö switch model/provider. |
| `FIX_AGENT_DESIGN` | An agent-construction bug (no tools / bad output contract) ŌĆö fix the op wiring. |
| `NEEDS_INVESTIGATION` | Unclassified failure ŌĆö inspect the sanitized diagnostic. |

### Verdict-schema field (the two verdict-returning gates only)
Add OPTIONAL `coverage.resolution_class` (enum, 8 values), `coverage.retryable` (bool), `coverage.diagnostic` (sanitized object) to `plan_review_verdict.schema.json` and `code_review_verdict.schema.json` (their `coverage` is already `additionalProperties:true`, so this is documentary; readers tolerate unknown keys ŌĆö forward/rollback-safe). **`completion_verdict.schema.json` is NOT changed** (shape B surfaces via the raised `.outcome`, no verdict dict ŌĆö a field there would be dead). Sole writer of the schema edits.

### Session-log diagnostic write (calling convention, both shapes)
At each degrade site (shape A helpers AND the shape-B `_completion_precheck` arm), call `rebar.append_session_log(entry, summary=ŌĆ”, relates_to=<ticket_id>)` where `entry = json.dumps(sanitized_diagnostic, sort_keys=True)` (the sanitized dict, never the raw exception), `summary = f"llm-degrade {resolution_class} on <gate>"`, `relates_to` = the reviewed ticket id when available. Wrapped best-effort (`try/except Exception`): a session-log failure must NEVER fail the gate (regression-tested).

### Never-sign guard + pre-sign fingerprint recheck (at the CALLER, not generic `sign_manifest`)
`signing.sign_manifest(ticket_id, manifest)` is a generic multi-caller primitive with no verdict in scope, so the guards live at the SIGN-DECISION callers:
- **Never-sign guard:** at the plan-review sign path (`review_plan`) and the completion sign path (`close_ticket`), refuse to CALL `sign_manifest` when `verdict.get("verdict") != "PASS"` OR `verdict.get("coverage",{}).get("resolution_class")` is set. This makes the existing "degraded ŌćÆ unsigned" invariant STRUCTURAL (regression-tested). It introduces no new unsigned state (INDETERMINATE is already unsigned).
- **Pre-sign fingerprint recheck:** "material fingerprint" = the committed head SHA the verdict was verified against (`signing.head_sha(repo_root)` / the attested `verified_at_sha`). In `close_ticket`, INSIDE the write lock, immediately before signing, re-read `head_sha` and compare to the verify-time value; on mismatch (code drifted during the retry/timeout-widened verifyŌåÆsign window) SKIP signing (close unsigned) rather than attest stale state. Verify itself stays OUTSIDE the write lock (`transition_close` ordering preserved; asserted).

### MCP structured failure return (`_mcp_llm.py`)
The write-gated MCP tools (`review_plan`, `review_code`, `verify_completion`) in `src/rebar/_mcp_llm.py` today `return rebar.llm.X(...)` and let a raised `LLMError`/`LLMUnavailableError` propagate as a FastMCP tool ERROR (opaque to the agent). Wrap each body: on a raised `LLMError`, RETURN a structured result dict `{"error": str(exc), "resolution_class": <cls or None>, "retryable": <bool>, "diagnostic": <sanitized>}` (disposition read from `getattr(exc,'outcome',None)`), so the driving agent can branch on `retryable` instead of parsing an error string. (Shape-A tools already return a degraded verdict dict; this closes the shape-B/hard-error raise.)

### Integration ownership (shared files)
runner.py was grown by five children to 824 LOC; this story owns its module-size reconciliation ŌĆö added to `.github/module-size-allowlist.txt` + `module-size-ceilings.txt` (850) + a `docs/architecture.md` remedy row (the concrete deliverable, verified by `test_module_size_contract.py`). Also owns the taxonomy/retry/timeout docs. Lands LAST.

## Scope
- `gate_dispatch.py` degrade write-sites; `code_review/shim.py` (`_verdict_to_review_result` coverage preservation); `transition_close.py` (`_completion_precheck` returncode-11 + shape-B session-log; `close_ticket` never-sign guard + in-lock fingerprint recheck) preserving verify-outside-lock ordering; `_llm_commands.py` exit-11 mapping (`_review_plan`/`_review_code`/`_verify_completion`); `_mcp_llm.py` structured-failure wrap; `signing.py` (only if a caller-side helper is factored ŌĆö the guard lives at the callers); the two verdict JSON schemas; `.github/module-size-*` + `docs/architecture.md`; `docs/exit-codes.md`; `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_exit_codes.py`; `docs/adr/`.
- NOT in scope: removing/weakening per-criterion fail-open; changing `completion_verdict.schema.json`; changing `sign_manifest`'s signature.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `_review_plan()` exits 11 when the verdict's `coverage.retryable` is true.
- [ ] `_review_plan()` exits 2 (existing INDETERMINATE) when INDETERMINATE and `coverage.retryable` is false/absent.
- [ ] `_review_code()` distinguishes `0/1/2/11` off the preserved `coverage` (it currently always returns 0).
- [ ] `shim._verdict_to_review_result` preserves `coverage.resolution_class`/`retryable`/`diagnostic` onto the returned `review_result`.
- [ ] `_completion_precheck` raises `CommandError(returncode=11)` when the caught exception's `.outcome.retryable` is true, else `returncode=1`.
- [ ] The `rebar close` / transition CLI path surfaces that returncode (exit 11) unchanged via `transition.py`.
- [ ] A degraded / INDETERMINATE verdict is emitted unsigned (never-sign guard at the caller refuses to call `sign_manifest`; regression test).
- [ ] The pre-sign head-SHA fingerprint is re-checked inside the write lock immediately before signing; a mismatch closes unsigned (test for the mismatch path).
- [ ] Verify remains OUTSIDE the write lock (`transition_close` ordering asserted by a test).
- [ ] A degraded verdict/close prints the class-specific message (from the table) to stderr.
- [ ] The sanitized diagnostic is written to the session log at BOTH the shape-A and shape-B degrade sites via `append_session_log(json.dumps(diagnostic, sort_keys=True), summary=ŌĆ”, relates_to=ŌĆ”)`, best-effort (a session-log failure does not fail the gate; regression test).
- [ ] `coverage.resolution_class` (enum), `coverage.retryable` (bool), `coverage.diagnostic` (object) are optional properties on `plan_review_verdict.schema.json` and `code_review_verdict.schema.json`; `completion_verdict.schema.json` is unchanged.
- [ ] The shape-A write-site copies `resolution_class`/`retryable`/`diagnostic` from the caught error's `.outcome` when present (outage paths); the string-error finders-failed path yields a plain INDETERMINATE.
- [ ] Per-criterion fail-open preserved: a single flaky finder among N does not block (regression test).
- [ ] `_mcp_llm.py`'s `review_plan`/`review_code`/`verify_completion` return a structured failure dict `{error,resolution_class,retryable,diagnostic}` on a raised `LLMError` instead of propagating a FastMCP tool error (test for a degraded sub-path).
- [ ] `docs/exit-codes.md` gains a recorded-decision entry defining exit 11; `tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_exit_codes.py` pins 11.
- [ ] The exit-11 ADR (sections: Context, Decision ŌĆö 11=block-but-retryable distinct from 2, Consumers+additive-safety, Rollback) enumerates consumers: the driving agent/human (docs/exit-codes.md), CI (`.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml`, `test.yml` ŌĆö any-non-zero-is-failure, never invokes the gate CLIs so 11 is invisible to Verified), the review-bot (`src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py` ŌĆö votes off the verdict dict, tolerates open coverage keys); each additive-safe; rollback = retryable falls back to the existing INDETERMINATE exit (no data migration).
- [ ] The diagnostic-sanitization ADR (sections: Context, Allowlist + redaction rules, Threat model ŌĆö keys/PII/hex/base64/email must never leak, Test coverage) is authored.
- [ ] runner.py is added to `.github/module-size-allowlist.txt` + `module-size-ceilings.txt` (850) + a `docs/architecture.md` remedy row (verified by `test_module_size_contract.py`).
- [ ] `make lint`, `make typecheck`, `make test` are all green.

## Verified structural facts (experiment log ŌĆö repo snapshot, offline)
```
Shape-B: transition_close.py::_completion_precheck (line ~174 broad except ŌåÆ CommandError from None discards .outcome; fix sets returncode from .outcome.retryable). CommandError (_commands/_seam.py) already carries returncode; transition.py returns exc.returncode (lines ~359/467).
Shape-A: gate_dispatch.py::_degraded_plan_review_verdict/_degraded_code_review_verdict. Called 3x: preflight (error=exc), mid-run (error=exc) ŌåÆ .outcome present; finders-failed (error=res.error STRING) ŌåÆ no .outcome ŌåÆ plain INDETERMINATE.
code-review CLI: rebar.llm.review_code ŌåÆ shim._verdict_to_review_result (translates verdictŌåÆreview_result; must preserve coverage). _review_code currently returns 0 for any verdict.
CLI arms: _llm_commands.py::_review_plan (tail 0/1/2), _review_code, _verify_completion. rebar close/transition ŌåÆ close_ticket ŌåÆ _completion_precheck.
Schemas (coverage additionalProperties:true): plan_review_verdict.schema.json, code_review_verdict.schema.json (edited); completion_verdict.schema.json unchanged.
Signing: signing.py::sign_manifest(ticket_id, manifest) generic multi-caller; guard + head_sha recheck live at callers (review_plan sign path, close_ticket) ŌĆö verify OUTSIDE write lock.
MCP: _mcp_llm.py review_plan/review_code/verify_completion return rebar.llm.X(...) (raise propagates as FastMCP error today).
Consumers: CI .github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml + test.yml (make targets, not gate CLIs); review-bot src/rebar/review_bot/adapter.py (verdict dict, not exit codes).
Exit contract: docs/exit-codes.md (0/1/2/10 frozen 2026-06-09) pinned by tests/interfaces/lifecycle/test_exit_codes.py; 11 additive.
```
Additive/rollback-safe: schema fields optional; exit 11 additive. Records the two NEW ADRs (exit-11, diagnostic-sanitization); transport-retry (0037) + async-watchdog (0038) already exist. Owns runner.py module-size reconciliation. Lands LAST.
